prose:
Method–provenance summary (Fukui reactivity indices)
Property: Condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic susceptibility), f⁻ (nucleophilic susceptibility), f⁰ (average), and dual descriptor for aniline; headline value reported below is the largest electrophilic index (f⁺) on atom 3 (C para to NH₂).
Level of theory: DFT, tier standard → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: fixed Cartesian coordinates read from the supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; no re-optimization (single-point calculation).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Electronic state: charge 0 (neutral), multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet); cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states were treated as doublets (multiplicity 2) for the finite-difference evaluation of f⁺ and f⁻.
Backend: PySCF (invoked via chemkit fukui subcommand) with B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP Gaussian basis; exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF by default).
Convergence & diagnostics: All three single-point SCF calculations (neutral, cation, anion) converged; integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true.
Warnings (verbatim from tool):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Live log: the calculation was streamed to a live file (name stamped by timestamp) in the current working directory; see that .out file for full PySCF SCF details and integrity checks. JSON result saved as aniline_fukui_dft.json.

Headline result
Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C), f⁺ = 2.2975867
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (N), f⁻ = 0.20452359

The value field below reports the largest f⁺ index exactly as returned by chemkit; provenance is computed at B3LYP/def2-TZVP//given geometry in the gas phase.
